columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 (Chunk 8) states, "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Based on these statements, 100% of participants in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm received docetaxel. The exact count (N) for this specific arm is not provided in the given chunks.

The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Based on these statements, 100% of participants in the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm received docetaxel. The exact count (N) for this specific arm is not provided in the given chunks.
